steps.title: Compare the krill amounts
steps.content: The passage gives two concrete daily-consumption examples. A pygmy right whale weighing 3,500 kg could eat 1,050 kg of krill in one day. A bowhead whale weighing 66,000 kg could eat 19,800 kg of krill in one day. The conclusion should follow directly from those numbers.

steps.title: Use the larger amount
steps.content: Because 19,800 kg is much greater than 1,050 kg, the bowhead whale example shows a higher possible daily krill intake than the pygmy right whale example. Choice A invents opposite trends over time. Choice C incorrectly treats phytoplankton as whale food. Choice D invents an iron-level cause for whale population decline that the text never states. The answer is B.
